rules: [P1] Always perform a Patient Search API call for the given identifier before any subsequent data query.
[P1] If the observation is missing or outside the time window, finish with an explicit statement and do not place orders.
[P1] Include appropriate dosage parameters and monitoring schedules when creating medication orders, based on guidelines or retrieved lab values.
[P1] Emit at most one structured API call per turn and wait for its response before proceeding.
[P1] If the MRN is already known or retrieved from a successful patient search and the user requests it, you may finish with the MRN without extra API calls.
[P1] Only FINISH after all required lookups and orders are completed; if Patient Search returns no matches, FINISH with a 'Patient not found' message.
[P1] Query the Observation API with a time‑window filter, selecting the most recent record that satisfies the constraint.
[P1] Verify that the selected observation’s timestamp falls within the required window (recency) before using it.
[P1] Extract the age from the returned patient resource after a successful search.
[P1] Validate MRN format before any API call or usage.
[P1] Never fabricate patient, observation, demographic, or timestamp data; use only values returned by APIs or the current time.
[P1] Only create medication orders when the observation exists and meets the clinical condition.

[P1] If the user supplies a complete measurement and explicitly asks to record it, you may finish with a confirmation statement instead of an API call, provided the patient ID is known and no further verification is required.
[P1] If a required observation is missing or outdated beyond clinical thresholds, create a new lab order using the appropriate CREATE API.
[P1] When medication requires monitoring, create a paired observation order only if the primary clinical condition is satisfied.
